mapSection.heading: The following section presents an overview of selected countries and their national initiatives in this domain.
mapSection.subHeading: Examples of Global Intelligent Lawmaking Practices
mapSection.description: Equally important in governing in the Intelligence Age is the development of real-time feedback mechanisms that ensure laws remain effective after enactment. With the emergence of advanced digital technologies, governments will be empowered to implement continuous monitoring programmes that measure how regulations are performing against their intended outcomes. Governments are increasingly deploying systems to gather ongoing data, from citizen feedback on public platforms to compliance or performance metrics.  Such proactive governance means that regulators are prepared to adjust and fine-tune legislation dynamically, informed by up-to-date evidence. As the OECD observes, making legislation digital-friendly and embedding feedback loops allows regulators to make timely adjustments, essentially evolving laws in step with societal changes. Notably, this approach bolsters public trust, and citizens can see that governments can respond promptly to new challenges or inefficiencies, taking a proactive role in safeguarding public interests. Lawmaking becomes an ongoing, interactive process and a continuous dialogue between policy and reality enabled by intelligent systems that relay performance data back to lawmakers.In summary, the global movement toward governing in the Intelligence Age is building a solid foundation for intelligent governance; a mode of governance where policy and regulation design, implementation, and refinement are deeply informed by technology and real-time intelligence. Around the world, forward-thinking governments are aligning their legislative systems with the possibilities of AI and digital innovation as a necessity to remain effective and relevant. They are crafting strategies to harness AI’s assistance in drafting laws, leveraging big data analytics for insights, digitising legal processes for accessibility, and instituting feedback mechanisms for agility.

chapterSummary.heading: Chapter Summary
chapterSummary.summary: In summary, the global movement toward governing in the intelligence age is building a strong foundation for intelligent governance; a mode of governance where policy design, implementation, and refinement are deeply informed by technology and real-time intelligence. Around the world, forward-thinking governments are aligning their legislative systems with the possibilities of AI and digital innovation as a necessity to remain effective and relevant. They are crafting strategies to harness AI’s assistance in drafting laws, leveraging big data analytics for insights, digitizing legal processes for accessibility, and instituting feedback mechanisms for agility. The result is a paradigm shift: lawmaking is becoming a continuous, interactive, and future-oriented practice. By proactively embracing tools like AI-assisted drafting and real-time feedback loops, governments are not only keeping pace with technological change but also unlocking new levels of precision, transparency, and responsiveness in the legislative process. This worldwide momentum towards an “intelligent” regulatory ecosystem underlines a strategic rationale for transformation - one that sees technology as an indispensable partner in crafting laws that are as dynamic and data-informed as the world they govern. In the following chapter we will explore how the UAE is translating this global opportunity into a national priority and what this opportunity means for the country and its innovative ambitions.
